summary: Solid first set, uneven second; highlights include Feel Like a Stranger and Last Time > Days > Ashes
blurb: The band opens strong with energetic playing and several standout tunes such as Feel Like A Stranger, Brown Eyed Women, and Easy Answers. The first set maintains high momentum while the second set feels uneven, though the Last Time > Days > Ashes space shines. Overall a mixed‑quality show worth listening to for its early highlights.
review:
San Diego Sports Arena on December 13, 1993 offered a night of highs and lows from the Dead. Jerry Garcia kicked off the evening with a bright, “Cripe”‑enhanced tone and solid vocal presence, especially on Feel Like A Stranger and Ramble On Rose, while his guitar work stayed tight through the first set’s fire‑cracker numbers like Brown Eyed Women and Easy Answers. Vince Welnick’s keyboards were a mixed bag—criticized during the soundcheck but praised later for an inspired Easy Answers solo and overall support. The rhythm section was generally solid; Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming suffered from a bad kick‑drum mic in one mix, yet his Drumz segment with Mickey Hart on Eyes Of The World remains a highlight. Phil Lesh stays in the background here, offering dependable bass foundation.

The second set loses some steam, with a sluggish LSD opener and a generally uneven flow, but the post‑space jam—The Last Time > Days > Ashes—delivers a soaring space adventure that lifts the night. The encore’s Liberty rounds things off on a modest note. While the show isn’t uniformly brilliant, its early set and select jams make it a worthwhile listen for fans seeking those 1993‑era gems.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the first set’s energy and specific songs (Feel Like A Stranger, Brown Eyed Women, Easy Answers, The Same Thing, Ramble On Rose, Eyes Of The World, Last Time > Days > Ashes). Though several reviews are mixed and one is negative about the soundcheck, the overall tone leans slightly positive, warranting a modest sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard capture with solid mix, preserving the strong first‑set energy and most of the highlighted moments.

key_highlights: First set delivers high‑energy performances with standout Feel Like A Stranger and Brown Eyed Women.
The Last Time > Days > Ashes space provides a soaring post‑space jam.
Vince Welnick shines on Easy Answers despite mixed keyboard reviews elsewhere.
Eyes Of The World/Drums segment showcases strong rhythmic interplay.

band_performance.Jerry: Energetic early with a new 'Cripe' effect, solid guitar tone and vocal presence; later shows signs of slipping but overall strong first‑set work.
band_performance.Bob: Strong opener on Stranger, gritty delivery on The Same Thing, confident vocals on Easy Answers; no major criticisms noted.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work receives mixed feedback—soundcheck criticized, yet praised for a standout Easy Answers solo and solid support elsewhere.
band_performance.Mickey: Generally solid percussion; highlighted drum segment in Eyes Of The World, though later drumming perceived as less impactful.
band_performance.Billy: Good overall drumming; kick‑drum mic issue noted in one mix but otherwise provides solid rhythmic foundation.
